published_prior_art.cite: BalkePearl1997: finite binary-IV response-type LP bounds; Picchetti2026: ATE/LATE identified sets under c-dependence and defier sensitivity

repo_prior_art.cite: none; closest pid_dynamic_iv_compliance_v2 targets dynamic assignment-memory mixture subtraction, not static three-coordinate IV ATE sign frontier

published_prior_art.cite: Picchetti2026 is closest but has only c-dependence plus defier sensitivity, not an independent never-taker cap
BaiHuangMoonShaikhVytlacil2024 studies no-power generalized monotonicity, not a never-taker redundancy threshold

structure_flags.label: Bibliography
structure_flags.one_line: The body names the Angrist--Evans family-size IV application, but Angrist and Evans (1998) is absent from the bibliography.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The stated inequality L(P;c,delta,nu) > L^square(P;c,delta,nu) is impossible as defined because Theta(P;0,delta,nu) is a subset of Theta(P;c,delta,nu), so L(P;0,delta,nu) >= L(P;c,delta,nu) and the max in L^square is at least the full endpoint.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: For an upper never-taker cap, endpoint redundancy requires at least one unconstrained minimizer with Pr(S=N) <= bar_nu, so the threshold is the infimum/minimum never-taker mass among minimizers, not the supremum rho_N as stated.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: For arbitrary Y in [ell,u] and full observable law P_{Y,D,Z}, replacing bounded outcomes by finite boundary response types at ell and u cannot preserve a non-binary observed outcome distribution; the theorem needs binary Y, finite observed support, or a measure-LP formulation.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Redefine the pairwise diagnostic so every pairwise model is comparable to the full sensitivity model; in particular, do not include the stronger c=0 independence model inside a lower-bound max meant to be weaker than the full c>0 model.

Replace rho_N with the minimum never-taker mass among unconstrained lower-endpoint minimizers, then state the redundancy certificate with a separate uniqueness or common-slack condition if a closed form needs the supremum.

Patch Theorem 1 by either restricting to binary or finite-support outcomes, or by stating compactness/attainment for probability measures while only boundary-discretizing unobserved counterfactual completions relevant to the linear ATE objective.
Add Angrist and Evans (1998) to the bibliography and use it as the named applied downstream consumer rather than only referring to the family-size application informally.

recommended_next_step: Pivot the statement-level geometry first: fix L^square monotonicity and the rho_N threshold, then resubmit the same novelty angle for a fresh review.
